question.text: A single premium life insurance policy is surrendered in the 2nd year of a 20-year term. The single premium paid was Rs. 5,00,000. What is the minimum guaranteed surrender value as per IRDAI regulations?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For single premium policies, the minimum guaranteed surrender value is 70% of the single premium if surrendered within the first 3 years. Since the policy is being surrendered in the 2nd year (within 3 years), the minimum surrender value = 70% x Rs. 5,00,000 = Rs. 3,50,000. The 90% threshold applies only in the last 2 years of the policy term. **The Trap:** 30% is the minimum surrender value for regular premium policies, not single premium policies. 90% applies only in the last 2 years of the policy term (years 19-20 in this case). Candidates who apply regular premium rules to single premium policies will get this wrong. **Cross-Exam Context:** Surrender value differences between single premium and regular premium policies are tested in NISM X-B and insurance-specific certifications. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Single premium policies have significantly higher surrender values (70% vs 30%) because the entire premium is paid upfront. This makes them more liquid, which can be a selling point for certain clients.
